cl_service_api_sh.name: Process API
cl_service_api_sh.description: The Processing API (or shortly "Process API") is the most commonly used API in Sentinel Hub as it provides images based on satellite data. Users can request raw satellite data, simple band combinations such as false colour composites, calculations of simple remote sensing indices like NDVI, or more advanced processing.

cl_service_api_sh.name: Catalog API
cl_service_api_sh.description: Sentinel Hub Catalog API (or shortly "Catalog") is an API implementing the STAC Specification, describing geospatial information about different data used with Sentinel Hub.

cl_service_api_sh.name: OGC Services
cl_service_api_sh.description: OGC services offer the access to the Sentinel Hub functionalists via interfaces, which conform to the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) standards: WMS, WCS, WFS, and WMTS.

cl_service_api_sh.name: Statistical API
cl_service_api_sh.description: The Statistical API (or shortly "Stats API") enables you to get statistics calculated based on satellite imagery without having to download images.

cl_service_api_sh.name: Batch Statistical API
cl_service_api_sh.description: The Batch Statistical API (or shortly "Batch Stats API") enables you to request statistics for multiple polygons at once and/or for longer aggregations.

cl_service_api_sh.name: Batch V2 Processing API
cl_service_api_sh.description: BatchV2 Processing API (or shortly "BatchV2 API") enables you to request data for large areas and/or longer time periods for any Sentinel Hub supported collection, including BYOC (bring your own data). It is an asynchronous REST service, meaning data won't be returned immediately but delivered to your specified object storage instead.

cl_service_api_sh.name: Synchronous OpenEO API
cl_service_api_sh.description: OpenEO service offers the API access to the standardized openEO graph processing with the Sentinel Hub data. The service is based on the openEO API standard and provides the capabilities to run the openEO process graphs on top of the Sentinel HUB raster processor results. The service is available for the Sentinel Hub users with the Sentinel Hub account and the access token.

cl_service_api.name: Jupyter Lab
cl_service_api.description: JupyterLab allows users to access their notebooks and use Jupyter Hub platform that provides an environment for creating, managing, and terminating Jupyter Notebooks.

cl_service_api.name: OData Download
cl_service_api.description: OData (Open Data Protocol) is a standard that specifies a variety of best practices for creating and using REST APIs. OData makes it possible to build REST-based data services that let Web clients publish and edit resources that are recognized by Uniform Resource Locators (URLs) and described in a data model using straightforward HTTP messages.

cl_service_api.name: Open Search
cl_service_api.description: The OpenSearch catalogue allows you to search through Copernicus data using a standardized web service. This web service returns results as GeoJSON feature collections. Each feature in the collection represents an earth observation "product", referencing where the actual data can be found.

cl_service_api.name: STAC
cl_service_api.description: STAC (Spatio-Temporal Asset Catalog) is a new web service specification for catalogues that is increasingly used and supported.

cl_service_api.name: Process API
cl_service_api.description: The Processing API (or shortly "Process API") is the most commonly used API in Sentinel Hub as it provides images based on satellite data. Users can request raw satellite data, simple band combinations such as false colour composites, calculations of simple remote sensing indices like NDVI, or more advanced processing.

cl_service_api.name: Asynchronous Processing API
cl_service_api.description: Asynchronous Processing API (or shortly "Async API") allows you to process more data with a single request than Processing API. This is possible because the processing results are not returned immediately but are - after some time - delivered in your object storage.

cl_service_api.name: Catalog API
cl_service_api.description: Sentinel Hub Catalog API (or shortly "Catalog") is an API implementing the STAC Specification, describing geospatial information about different data used with Sentinel Hub.

cl_service_api.name: OGC Services
cl_service_api.description: OGC services offer the access to the Sentinel Hub functionalists via interfaces, which conform to the Open Geospatial Consortium (OGC) standards: WMS, WCS, WFS, and WMTS.

cl_service_api.name: Statistical API
cl_service_api.description: The Statistical API (or shortly "Stats API") enables you to get statistics calculated based on satellite imagery without having to download images.

cl_service_api.name: Batch statistical API
cl_service_api.description: The Batch Statistical API (or shortly "Batch Stats API") enables you to request statistics for multiple polygons at once and/or for longer aggregations.

cl_service_api.name: Batch V2 Processing API
cl_service_api.description: BatchV2 Processing API (or shortly "BatchV2 API") enables you to request data for large areas and/or longer time periods for any Sentinel Hub supported collection, including BYOC (bring your own data). It is an asynchronous REST service, meaning data won't be returned immediately but delivered to your specified object storage instead.

cl_service_api.name: BYOC API
cl_service_api.description: Bring Your Own COG API (or shortly "BYOC") enables you to import your own data in Sentinel Hub and access it just like any other data you are used to.

cl_service_api.name: Zarr Import API
cl_service_api.description: Zarr Import API (or shortly "Zarr") enables you to import your own Zarr data in Sentinel Hub and access it just like any other data under some conditions.

cl_service_api.name: Synchronous OpenEO API
cl_service_api.description: OpenEO service offers the API access to the standardized openEO graph processing with the Sentinel Hub data. The service is based on the openEO API standard and provides the capabilities to run the openEO process graphs on top of the Sentinel HUB raster processor results. The service is available for the Sentinel Hub users with the Sentinel Hub account and the access token.

cl_service_api.name: ODP Service
cl_service_api.description: The On-Demand Processing API with OData interface allows the users to interact with the service to issue and control the orders. It provides functionalities like creation, update, cancellation, pausing and monitoring of orders. This allows the users to have a better control over the workflow execution process.

cl_service_api.name: ODP User interface
cl_service_api.description: The Ecosystem Workspace provides the user interface to the On-Demand Processing service. It allows selection of input data, as well as creation, parametrization and monitoring of orders.

cl_service_api.name: ODP API Sentinel-1
cl_service_api.description: Sentinel-1 On-Demand Processing (ODP) is a serverless data computation service that triggers the generation of higher-level user products (usually Level 1, 2 or 3) from archived lower-level products (typically Level 0) for those that are not available online.

cl_service_api.name: ODP API Sentinel-2
cl_service_api.description: Sentinel-2 On-Demand Processing (ODP) is a serverless data computation service that triggers the generation of higher-level user products (usually Level 1, 2 or 3) from archived lower-level products (typically Level 0) for those that are not available online.

cl_service_api.name: ODP API Sentinel-3
cl_service_api.description: Sentinel-3 On-Demand Processing (ODP) is a serverless data computation service that triggers the generation of higher-level user products (usually Level 1, 2 or 3) from archived lower-level products (typically Level 0) for those that are not available online.

cl_service_api.name: Zipper API availability
cl_service_api.description: The Zipper API allows users downloading for entire EO Data products in their original form.

cl_service_api.name: S3 Object Data Access API
cl_service_api.description: Access to EO data hosted on object storage is using API compatible with S3. S3 is an object storage service with which you users can retrieve data over HTTP using REST API.

cl_service_api.name: openEO Algorithm Plaza
cl_service_api.description: The openEO algorithm plaza simplifies sharing your developed algorithms with our community, removing technical hurdles so you can focus on development. It boosts visibility by making your algorithm accessible through user-friendly web interfaces, complete with visual descriptions of its capabilities.

cl_service_api.name: Copernicus Browser
cl_service_api.description: The Copernicus Browser is a web browser application that allows you to easily search, visualize, modify and download imagery from the Sentinel satellites.

cl_service_api.name: Traceability User Interface
cl_service_api.description: The Traceability User Interface allows users to query all available traces in the Ecosystem to search for a product's traces and verify the origin of the downloaded product.

cl_service_api.name: Traceability Service
cl_service_api.description: Traceability Service provides the user with means to track the lifecycle of a data product. It acts as a historian of the product’s lifecycle, collecting the traces of all related events.

cl_service_api.name: openEO API
cl_service_api.description: The openEO standardized API enables you to access and process earth observation datasets in the ecosystem using intuitive programming libraries. The processing is automatically scaled on the cloud infrastructure that also hosts the data. This allows for a range of use cases, from explorative research to large-scale production of EO-derived maps and information.

cl_service_api.name: openEO Web Editor
cl_service_api.description: The openEO Web Editor is a web app that allows users to interact with an openEO back-end and perform various tasks related to Earth observation data processing. This application can be of great use for users who are not familiar with a programming language.

cl_timeliness.name: Sentinel-1 NRT
cl_timeliness.description: Publication timeliness is measured as the time between the satellite observation and the time at which the resulting data is discoverable to users. Products with Near Real Time (NRT) timeliness are processed shortly after acquisition, using auxiliary information available at the time of the data acquisition on ground.

cl_timeliness.name: Sentinel-1 NTC
cl_timeliness.description: Publication timeliness is measured as the time between the satellite observation and the time at which the resulting data is discoverable to users. Products with Non Time Critical (NTC) timeliness are processed with a predefined delay after sensing, typically waiting for the most accurate version of the necessary auxiliary data.

cl_timeliness.name: Sentinel-2 NTC
cl_timeliness.description: Publication timeliness is measured as the time between the satellite observation and the time at which the resulting data is discoverable to users. Products with Non Time Critical (NTC) timeliness are processed with a predefined delay after sensing, typically waiting for the most accurate version of the necessary auxiliary data.

cl_timeliness.name: Sentinel-3 SRAL STC

Products with Short Time Critical (STC) timeliness are processed typically after several hours or days with consolidated auxiliary information.

cl_timeliness_default.name: Sentinel-1 Default Timeliness
cl_timeliness_default.description: Publication timeliness is measured as the time between the satellite observation and the time at which the resulting data is discoverable to users. Default timeliness is a common timeliness applicable to all mission data.
